title: Why doesnt Tabris want to destroy man kind like all the rest
display_title: Why doesnt Tabris want to destroy man kind like all the rest
wikitext:
[]: Why [doesn't] Tabris want to destroy man kind [like] all the rest?             Quote Post by TraxXavier » Fri Dec 11, 2015 9:54 am Why do Tabris dont want to destroy man kind unlike all the rest of the angels? Granted he was reborn in a human form for a few years, but lets be honest a measly decade or so is nothing in comparation to how ever long he has probably lived before. And unlike Rei he appears to remember his existence as Adam. So why would he in so few years change his opinion about man kind?

[]: Re: Why do Tabris dont want to destroy man kind unlike all the rest?             Quote Post by pwhodges » Fri Dec 11, 2015 10:06 am Because of the qualities he sees and comes to recognise in Shinji, however you want to describe them (love, potential, ...). He came to subvert Shinji, but ended up being subverted by him. It's harder to explain in Rebuild, as we haven't (I suspect) got the whole of that aspect of the story yet.

[]: Re: Why do Tabris dont want to destroy man kind unlike all the rest?             Quote Post by TraxXavier » Fri Dec 11, 2015 10:09 am idk.. i mean when he came to NERV he was suposed to serve SEELE's agenda. And this one must have been different from the agenda of the angels, as otherwhice, why bothering creating NERV etc. just do nothing and wait. although iirc he thought he would be reuniting with his old body and not with lilith, that was a point where seele tricked him, but why would that mater. Or asked differently what would be his agenda going to NERV initially.

[]: Re: Why do Tabris dont want to destroy man kind unlike all the rest?             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Dec 11, 2015 10:26 am TraxXavier wrote: Granted he was reborn in a human form for a few years, but lets be honest a measly decade or so is nothing in comparation to how ever long he has probably lived before. And unlike Rei he appears to remember his existence as Adam. So why would he in so few years change his opinion about man kind?

[]: Re: Why do Tabris dont want to destroy man kind unlike all the rest?             Quote Post by pwhodges » Fri Dec 11, 2015 12:03 pm It seems to me that neither Rei nor Kaworu is able to express their full nature when constrained within a Lilin body. Given that, I prefer an explanation that works adequately within canon to one which might seem more convincing but involves speculation (such as an off-screen deal with Yui).

[]: Re: Why do Tabris dont want to destroy man kind unlike all the rest?             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Dec 11, 2015 3:56 pm TraxXavier wrote: Why do Tabris dont want to destroy man kind unlike all the rest of the angels?

[]: Re: Why do Tabris dont want to destroy man kind unlike all the rest?             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Dec 11, 2015 6:17 pm pwhodges wrote: It seems to me that neither Rei nor Kaworu is able to express their full nature when constrained within a Lilin body. Given that, I prefer an explanation that works adequately within canon to one which might seem more convincing but involves speculation (such as an off-screen deal with Yui).

[]: Re: Why do Tabris dont want to destroy man kind unlike all the rest?             Quote Post by Rei IV » Fri Dec 11, 2015 9:40 pm Reichu wrote: I've very frequently wondered the same thing, myself. Abandoning a mission that involved self-sacrifice in the name of one's species, on the scale of BILLIONS OF YEARS ALL ALONE , all on account of less than a day , with one kid who was previously a stranger, is a pretty hard pill to swallow. The only options I can come up with: (A) Kaworu did not really sacrifice his life for Shinji's sake. Those were just pretty, insincere words intended to manipulate the feeble Lilin child and disguise from him what was actually going on. Or something along those lines. (This can tie into the idea that Kaworu made an off-screen deal with Yui to take his and the Angel's souls off the planet with her.) (B) Suppose the whole point of Seele putting Adam into a Lilin body was to make her more controllable. A young human is impressionable, and the "reincarnation" process could have easily resulted in some amount of dissociation from Kaworu's true self. Seems a fine way to brainwash a god into one's little pet and make them lose sight of what should truly matter. (C) Adam should have never been a Seed, as she apparently lacks the spiritual fortitude for it.

[]: Re: Why do Tabris dont want to destroy man kind unlike all the rest?             Quote Post by Dream » Sat Dec 12, 2015 2:08 pm Reichu wrote: I've very frequently wondered the same thing, myself. Abandoning a mission that involved self-sacrifice in the name of one's species, on the scale of BILLIONS OF YEARS ALL ALONE , all on account of less than a day , with one kid who was previously a stranger, is a pretty hard pill to swallow.

[]: Re: Why do Tabris dont want to destroy man kind unlike all the rest?             Quote Post by Ieyasu » Fri Dec 25, 2015 9:41 pm Doesn't he say something like "Whether I live or die makes no great difference. In truth death may be the only absolute freedom there is." Maybe admiration for Shinji's tenacity, since the kid clearly is afraid of living and dying but still holds on looking for reasons. Maybe Kaworu's experience of and knowledge of existence was such that as a potential immortal at some point immortality would render eternal life meaningless anyway. And like he says, maybe he was trapped too, and death would be the release he would not have if he won. I don't know a lot about the angels but I have it in my head that their existence is different from humankind's. Or maybe it's just as depressing and difficult for them too. Tabris is part angel and human so he'd have at least some understanding of human life and maybe he came to prefer it to angel life. I 'UNNO.

[]: Re: Why [doesn't] Tabris want to destroy man kind [like] all the rest?             Quote Post by Tumbling Down » Mon Dec 28, 2015 1:14 am Because mankind has crunchy peanut butter.

[]: Re: Why [doesn't] Tabris want to destroy man kind [like] all the rest?             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Dec 28, 2015 1:20 am The title was killing me, I just had to fix it.
